title: Validating Git Ref Names with Special Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a given Git ref name. A valid Git ref name must match the pattern `^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$` and must not exceed 256 characters in length. The function should return `true` if the ref name is valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git ref name to be validated.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the ref name is valid.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input string only contains ASCII characters.

constraints.description: Maximum allowed length of the ref name

examples.explanation: The ref name is valid and within the allowed length.
The ref name exceeds the maximum allowed length.
The ref name contains a non-alphanumeric character.

hints.text: Use a regular expression to check if the ref name matches the pattern.

editorial.title: Editorial for Validating Git Ref Names with Special Characters
editorial.intuition: The intuition behind this problem is to validate the ref name against a specific pattern and check if it exceeds the maximum allowed length.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve iterating over each character in the ref name and checking if it matches the pattern. However, this approach would be inefficient and may not be able to handle large input strings.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach would be to use a regular expression to check if the ref name matches the pattern. This approach is more efficient and can handle large input strings.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a regular expression to check if the ref name matches the pattern and checking if it exceeds the maximum allowed length. This solution is both efficient and correct.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the regular expression used correctly matches the pattern and that the length check is correct.
editorial.dryRun: Here is a dry run of the solution: input = 'refs/heads/master', output = true.
commonMistakes: Not checking the length of the ref name
